notes: Inventory only. No integration, packaging, hook execution, or source mutation was performed.
Nested source directories do not contain local .git metadata, so commit/tag values are marked unverified unless a local manifest states them.

expected_sources.name: claude-overlay
expected_sources.purpose: Claude-oriented overlay of system prompts, tool descriptions, and migration/reference materials.
expected_sources.source_url_verification: inferred from local CHANGELOG links; no local git remote
risks: Very large permanent prompt corpus; direct import would overload a Codex-first core if copied wholesale.
Content is Claude-specific and includes tool/model semantics that do not map 1:1 to Codex.
Contains examples of destructive commands in prompt text; safe as documentation, risky if transplanted without filtering.
possible_use: Reference-only source for migration patterns, safety wording, and prompt decomposition.
Do not import raw always-on instructions into Codex without normalization.
redistribution_constraints: MIT is permissive, but preserve notices.
Review third-party references before bundling excerpts.

expected_sources.name: subagents
expected_sources.purpose: Large catalog of Claude-style subagent definitions grouped by category.
expected_sources.source_url_verification: stated in local README; no local git remote
risks: Agent format is Claude frontmatter, not native Codex agent TOML.
Many agents grant broad tool sets in Claude terms; write scope must be re-derived for Codex.
Catalog is oversized for default inclusion and has semantic overlap with existing skills-pack agents.
possible_use: Selective extraction of a small Codex-native agent set.
Reference pool for role wording and completion criteria.
redistribution_constraints: MIT is permissive, but preserve notices.
Do not auto-install or auto-enable third-party installer flows.

expected_sources.name: skills-pack
expected_sources.purpose: Current integrated Codex skills pack and agent bundle, now expanded with SEO, marketing, content-studio, and editorial workflows.
expected_sources.source_url_verification: unverified; local README describes prior integrations but no repo remote metadata is present
risks: License model is heterogeneous; root-level license is absent and redistribution must follow per-skill notices.
Several skills reference platform-specific or potentially destructive shell examples; these need allowlisting and routing discipline.
Already contains design, review, browser, deployment, and Caveman workflows, so new additions can duplicate behavior if merged indiscriminately.
possible_use: Primary base for the final Codex distribution.
Source of existing routing, validation, notices, and current Caveman integration.
redistribution_constraints: Honor per-skill source licenses and notice files.
Do not assume the entire pack can be relicensed under one umbrella.

expected_sources.name: caveman
expected_sources.purpose: Expected by master prompt as a standalone source, but already represented inside skills-pack.
risks: Standalone provenance cannot be re-verified from sources/caveman because the directory is absent.
possible_use: Treat as covered by the already integrated Caveman skills inside skills-pack unless the user later adds a standalone source.
For planning, model this as an embedded capability rather than a blocking missing source.

expected_sources.name: graphify
expected_sources.purpose: Knowledge-graph tooling plus host-specific skill generators and install/update flows.
expected_sources.source_url_verification: verified from local source-manifest.json and pyproject
expected_sources.commit_or_tag_verification: from local source-manifest.json; repository .git metadata absent
dependencies: optional extras: mcp, starlette, neo4j, falkordb, pypdf, markdownify, watchdog, openpyxl, faster-whisper, yt-dlp, openai, tiktoken, anthropic, boto3, jieba, tree-sitter-sql, tree-sitter-dm, tree-sitter-hcl
risks: README promotes `curl | sh` for uv installation; this conflicts with the master prompt safety policy.
Graphify ships many platform-specific skills and hooks; direct import would duplicate functionality already present in skills-pack.
Codex integration depends on exact CLI behavior and optional features that must be verified before wiring into core.
possible_use: Optional integration layer and dedicated Graphify wrapper skill.
Reference for install/update/doctor/uninstall flows after exact commands are verified.
redistribution_constraints: MIT is permissive, but preserve notices.
Generated or packaged dependencies may carry separate licenses outside the repository.

expected_sources.name: project-rules
expected_sources.purpose: First-party Codex-native source of project rules and migration-safe support material.
possible_use: Primary first-party home for Codex-native core policy and migration-safe support material.
Used to keep clean authored policy separate from imported upstream sources.

expected_sources.name: codebase-memory-mcp
expected_sources.purpose: Local code intelligence MCP source used through a project-scoped Powerpack adapter.
expected_sources.source_url_verification: declared in local README and server.json
expected_sources.commit_or_tag_verification: version declared locally; commit unverified because .git is absent
executables: release binary is acquired separately; none bundled in dist
hooks: upstream supports client hooks; Powerpack does not install them
dependencies: source build requires C/C++ toolchain; release binary is static
risks: upstream install/update/uninstall mutate multiple user-level agent configurations
upstream default cache is user-global unless CBM_CACHE_DIR is set
UI and watcher require explicit policy
possible_use: project-scoped stdio MCP for code symbols, graphs, imports, diffs, architecture and impact analysis
redistribution_constraints: preserve MIT license and third-party notices
verify downloaded release binaries by SHA-256

instruction_conflicts.details: subagents are authored for Claude frontmatter and tool names, while skills-pack custom agents already target Codex TOML.
Graphify and Claude-derived materials use slash commands and Claude/Cursor style always-on instructions; the target pack must route through Codex-native skills and AGENTS fragments instead.
claude-overlay contains many long system prompts that should stay as references, not always-on core policy.

missing_tool_references: Graphify README references uv installation via shell bootstrap and multiple optional platform installers that are not verified in this workspace.
subagents README references Claude plugin marketplace and curl-based installers that are not suitable for direct Codex integration.
